summary: This glossary defines common mortgage terms in plain language to help borrowers understand the process. It is education-only and does not provide personalized pricing, eligibility decisions, or application processing.

key_steps.title: Look up unfamiliar terms
key_steps.instruction: When you see an unfamiliar term in a lender document, look it up and note where it appears so you can ask questions with context.

key_steps.title: Ask for clarification when needed
key_steps.instruction: If a definition does not match how a term is used in your specific document, ask your Loan Officer or closing agent to clarify.

faq_items.question: What is APR?
faq_items.answer: APR is a standardized measure that can reflect certain costs of borrowing expressed as a rate. For any questions about your exact APR or pricing, speak directly with your Loan Officer.

faq_items.question: What is escrow?
faq_items.answer: Escrow commonly refers to a process or account used to manage certain payments, such as taxes and insurance, depending on lender requirements and your loan setup.

faq_items.question: What is underwriting?
faq_items.answer: Underwriting is generally a review stage where documentation is checked according to lender requirements. It may include follow-up questions or additional document requests.

escalation_triggers.trigger_text: What is my APR or rate?
Do these terms mean I qualify?
